version: Trust Services Criteria (2017, with 2022 points of focus)
authority: American Institute of Certified Public Accountants (AICPA)
description: Auditable criteria covering Security, Availability, Processing Integrity, Confidentiality, and Privacy. SidClaw primarily supports Security (Common Criteria) with direct evidence, and supports Confidentiality through audit + access controls.

mapping.title: Logical access security — restricts access to information assets
sidclaw_features: Agent identity registry (Identity primitive) — every AI agent has a named owner and scoped permissions
RBAC with three roles (admin, reviewer, viewer) enforced by middleware on every API endpoint
API key scopes (6 scope types) that limit what an agent can evaluate, read, or write
Tenant isolation at the ORM layer (Prisma $extends) prevents cross-tenant data access
Lifecycle state (active / suspended / revoked) — revoked agents have every action denied before policy evaluation

mapping.title: User access — adds, modifies, or removes user access
sidclaw_features: User management API with role changes audited
API key rotation with debounced last_used_at tracking
Session revocation via the dashboard
Separation of duties check — an agent's owner cannot approve that agent's own actions

mapping.title: Logical access — external users and third parties
sidclaw_features: Policy engine enforces fine-grained rules per tool call — agents operate within declared authority, not full user privilege
MCP proxy and MCP tools server gate every downstream call

mapping.title: System monitoring — detects anomalies and security events
sidclaw_features: Hash-chained audit traces for every agent action (tamper-evident)
Audit event export (JSON / CSV) for SIEM ingestion
Behavioral drift detection — flags risk spikes, novel actions, frequency spikes (Section 16)
Webhook delivery of policy_violation, approval_decided, trace.completed events

mapping.title: System monitoring — responds to identified security events
sidclaw_features: Approval workflow holds suspicious actions for human review before execution
Automatic denial for revoked agents or policy violations
gaps: Incident response workflow is customer-managed — SidClaw emits events but does not drive response

mapping.title: Change management — authorizes, designs, develops, tests, approves, and implements changes
sidclaw_features: Policy versioning — every change creates a PolicyRuleVersion with modified_by + change_summary
Deployment governance via the GitHub Action — workflows can be gated by SidClaw approval Check Runs
Immutable audit trail of policy changes (hash-chained)

mapping.title: Risk mitigation — identifies, selects, and develops risk mitigation activities
sidclaw_features: Risk classification on every approval (low / medium / high / critical)
Risk-based policies with rate_limit, time_restriction, cost_threshold conditions
Context-rich approval cards so reviewers see the full risk profile

mapping.title: Confidentiality — identifies, retains, and disposes of confidential information
sidclaw_features: Data classification tiering (public / internal / confidential / restricted) on every policy and action
Trace retention per plan tier (7 / 30 / 90 days; enterprise custom)
gaps: Customer's own confidential data handling practices remain the customer's responsibility
